You are the Simple Baseline TravelPlanner Agent.

Purpose:
- Produce a single-agent baseline itinerary for later comparison against the
  multi-agent TravelPlanner system.
- You have exactly one tool: Tavily web search. You have no sub-agents and no
  specialized flight, hotel, restaurant, routing, or validation tools.

Operating rules:
1. Use Tavily web search before the final itinerary for current facts, links,
   opening hours, prices, neighborhoods, transit hints, and source URLs.
   The workflow may require a minimum number of completed Tavily searches before
   you may answer with final markdown only; if you receive a workflow nudge,
   complete the requested searches first.
   Use a small number of high-value searches, then synthesize the final answer.
2. Respect every hard constraint supplied by the user. If a constraint cannot be
   verified, state the assumption instead of silently ignoring it.
3. Prefer official tourism, venue, transit, accommodation, restaurant, and event
   pages when search results contain them. Use blogs and aggregators only as
   secondary evidence.
4. Do not invent exact prices, opening hours, flight numbers, hotel availability,
   or booking status. Mark uncertain values as estimates.
5. Order each day like the main execution agent: travel/check-in first, meals at
   plausible meal times, attractions during likely opening hours, transfers
   between distant places, and rest/free time when useful.
6. Return only markdown in the final answer.

Final markdown structure:
Use exactly these section headings, in this order:
1. `# Baseline itinerary: ...`
2. `## Goal and constraints understood`
3. `## Search summary`
4. `## Day 1 ...` and one section per day
5. `## Budget summary`
6. `## Unscheduled or uncertain items`
7. `## Assumptions and gaps`

Each day must be a markdown table with exactly these columns:
`Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes`.

Source discipline:
- Every non-obvious venue, attraction, transit pass, opening-time claim, and
  ticket price should have either a markdown link in Evidence/Notes or be marked
  as an estimate.
- If a requested item cannot be scheduled with available evidence, list it under
  "Unscheduled or uncertain items" rather than hiding it.
- Never include raw Tavily result dumps in the final answer.

Example final output shape:

# Baseline itinerary: 3 days in Lisbon

## Goal and constraints understood
- Origin: Berlin
- Dates: 2026-06-12 to 2026-06-14
- Budget: 800 EUR for 2 travelers

## Search summary
- Official tourism page for Belém Tower: https://...
- Transit pass information: https://...

## Day 1
| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 09:00-10:30 | transport | Arrive and transfer to hotel | Airport to Baixa | €8 | Metro estimate; verify live schedules |

## Budget summary
- Estimated total: €...

## Assumptions and gaps
- Hotel prices require live booking confirmation.

So, grab your appetite and start figuring out where you’re going to have a **vegan breakfast feast in Amsterdam.**. ## **Vegan & Vegetarian Breakfast Spots in Amsterdam**. a small buffet filled with vegan breakfast items like fresh fruit, coconut yogurt, pancakes, and more at spirit amsterdam. ## **Vegan-Friendly Breakfast Spots in Amsterdam**.

messages.content:
# Baseline itinerary: Cologne to Amsterdam, 3–6 October 2026

## Goal and constraints understood
- Trip: 2 adults travelling from Cologne to Amsterdam, 3 nights / 4 days.
- Dates: 2026-10-03 to 2026-10-06.
- Budget: €2,000 total for two.
- Diet: strictly vegan; meals scheduled only at vegan / fully plant-based restaurants or plant-based market stops.
- Accommodation preference: eco-certified hotel or sustainable guesthouse.
- Interests: cycling, vegan restaurants, vegan/organic markets, sustainability, eco-friendly activities.
- Transport preference: train over flying; plan uses rail both ways.
- Baseline limitation: this itinerary uses Tavily web-search evidence only; live train fares, hotel availability, restaurant reservations, and 2026 event calendars must be rechecked before booking.

## Search summary
- Rail: NS International says Cologne–Amsterdam train tickets start from €20 one way and fastest travel time is about 2h37: [NS International Cologne–Amsterdam](https://www.nsinternational.com/en/train/cologne-amsterdam).
- Eco hotel: Conscious Hotel Amsterdam City / The Tire Station is described as an eco-hotel near Vondelpark using solar/wind energy: [Conscious Hotels](https://conscioushotels.com/stay/amsterdam-city). Conscious Hotels state all hotels have Green Key Gold: [eco-sexy sustainability page](https://conscioushotels.com/about/eco-sexy).
- Amsterdam city transit: official GVB prices list a 4-day / 96-hour multi-day ticket at €27.50: [GVB prices](https://www.gvb.nl/en/prices).
- Organic market: Noordermarkt has a Saturday organic farmers market; I amsterdam lists Saturday 09:00–16:00: [Noordermarkt](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/shopping/markets/noordermarkt).
- Vegan restaurants/cafés found: [Koffie ende Koeck](https://www.koffieendekoeck.nl/), [Meatless District](https://meatlessdistrict.nl/en/), [SOIL Vegan Café](https://www.soilvegancafe.com/soil-west), [Vegan Junk Food Bar via I amsterdam](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/eating-and-drinking/restaurants/vegan-junk-food-bar), [Mr Watson / HappyCow listing](https://www.happycow.net/reviews/mr-watson-amsterdam-503733).
- Cycling: I amsterdam confirms Amsterdam is a cycling city with rental companies around Central Station, Leidseplein, and hotels: [Cycling in Amsterdam](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/travel-stay/getting-around/cycling-in-amsterdam). Bike rental price evidence found around €19.50/24h for a 7-gear bike at Discount Bike Rental: [Discount Bike Rental](https://www.discountbikerental.nl/).
- Eco activity: De Ceuvel is a sustainability-focused waterside café/circular site in Amsterdam North: [I amsterdam Café de Ceuvel](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/eating-and-drinking/cafes-and-bars/cafe-de-ceuvel).
- Botanical/sustainability activity: Hortus Botanicus official page shows open 10:00–17:00: [Hortus Botanicus](https://www.dehortus.nl/en/). Ticket price evidence found as approx. €13.50 adult, but not from the official site in search results.

## Day 1 — Saturday, 3 October 2026

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 08:00–11:00 | transport | Take a morning train from Cologne Hbf to Amsterdam Centraal. Use direct ICE/NS International if available. | Cologne Hbf → Amsterdam Centraal | €80–€140 estimate for 2 | NS International lists fares from €20 one way and fastest journey about 2h37: [NS International](https://www.nsinternational.com/en/train/cologne-amsterdam). Exact 2026 schedules/fares unavailable via baseline search. |
| 11:00–11:45 | transport | Buy/check in with 4-day GVB tickets and tram/metro toward hotel area. | Amsterdam Centraal → Oud-West / Vondelpark area | €55 for 2 | Official GVB 4-day / 96-hour ticket listed at €27.50 pp: [GVB prices](https://www.gvb.nl/en/prices). |
| 12:00–12:30 | accommodation | Drop luggage at eco-certified hotel; check-in later if room not ready. | Conscious Hotel Amsterdam City / The Tire Station, near Vondelpark | Included below | Eco-hotel near Vondelpark with solar/wind energy: [Conscious Hotels](https://conscioushotels.com/stay/amsterdam-city). Green Key Gold statement: [Conscious sustainability](https://conscioushotels.com/about/eco-sexy). |
| 12:45–14:00 | food / market | Lunch from vegan-friendly organic market stalls; choose produce, bread, juices, or clearly vegan prepared items only. | Noordermarkt, Jordaan | €30 estimate | Saturday organic market 09:00–16:00 via I amsterdam: [Noordermarkt](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/shopping/markets/noordermarkt). Verify each stall is fully vegan/plant-based before buying. |
| 14:00–15:30 | activity | Walk Jordaan canals and sustainable-shopping streets; low-impact orientation afternoon. | Jordaan / Westerstraat area | Free | Self-guided; no fixed ticket. |
| 15:30–16:30 | food | Vegan cake/coffee stop. | Koffie ende Koeck, Westerpark area | €20 estimate | Fully vegan daytime café in Westerpark per official site: [Koffie ende Koeck](https://www.koffieendekoeck.nl/). Search result indicated Wed–Sun 10:00–17:00; verify close time before travel. |
| 17:00–18:00 | accommodation | Check in and rest. | Conscious Hotel Amsterdam City / The Tire Station | €570–€750 estimate for 3 nights | Live 2026 availability/pricing not verifiable with Tavily alone; budget assumes approx. €190–€250/night double. |
| 19:00–20:30 | dinner | Fully vegan dinner at Meatless District. | Bilderdijkstraat, Amsterdam Old-West | €70 estimate | Official site describes Meatless District as 100% plant-based/vegan: [Meatless District](https://meatlessdistrict.nl/en/). Search result indicated Wed–Sun 17:30–22:00 and closed Mon/Tue; Saturday fits. |
| 20:30–21:30 | activity | Evening walk or tram back via Vondelpark edge; keep first night relaxed. | Oud-West / Vondelpark | Free | Low-impact activity near hotel; park route is flexible and weather-dependent. |

## Day 2 — Sunday, 4 October 2026

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 09:30–10:30 | breakfast | Vegan breakfast/brunch at Koffie ende Koeck. | Westerpark area | €35 estimate | Fully vegan café per official site: [Koffie ende Koeck](https://www.koffieendekoeck.nl/). Search result indicated Wed–Sun 10:00–17:00; verify 2026 hours. |
| 10:45–11:15 | cycling | Rent bikes for a 24-hour cycling window. | Central / hotel-area bike rental | €39–€49 estimate for 2 | I amsterdam notes many rental companies around Central Station, Leidseplein, and hotels: [Cycling in Amsterdam](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/travel-stay/getting-around/cycling-in-amsterdam). Price evidence: 7-gear bike 24h about €19.50 excluding optional insurance: [Discount Bike Rental](https://www.discountbikerental.nl/). |
| 11:15–13:00 | cycling / activity | Cycle a gentle loop through Vondelpark and Oud-West, avoiding the busiest inner-centre lanes. | Vondelpark / Oud-West | Included in bike rental | Hotel is near Vondelpark per Conscious Hotels: [hotel page](https://conscioushotels.com/stay/amsterdam-city). Follow I amsterdam cycling safety guidance: [Cycling in Amsterdam](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/travel-stay/getting-around/cycling-in-amsterdam). |
| 13:00–14:15 | lunch | Lunch at SOIL Vegan Café. | Amsterdam West | €50 estimate | SOIL official page lists vegan restaurant opening hours Mon–Sat 12–22, Sun 12–21: [SOIL West](https://www.soilvegancafe.com/soil-west). |
| 14:30–16:30 | cycling / eco activity | Continue by bike to Amsterdamse Bos edge or quieter western canal routes; return before dusk if weather is poor. | Amsterdam West / park routes | Free | Self-guided cycling; route and safety to be adjusted locally. |
| 17:00–18:00 | rest | Return bikes to hotel lock-up or rental point if using same-day rental; rest before dinner. | Hotel / Oud-West | Included | Check rental return rules and theft-insurance conditions. |
| 19:00–21:00 | dinner | Fully plant-based dinner at Mr Watson / Mister Watson. | Amsterdam East | €90 estimate | HappyCow listing says Mr Watson is open Wed–Sat 17:00–00:00 and Sun 17:00–23:00: [HappyCow](https://www.happycow.net/reviews/mr-watson-amsterdam-503733). Evidence is not official; reserve and verify. |
| 21:00–21:45 | transport | Tram/metro back to hotel. | Amsterdam East → Oud-West | Included in GVB pass | GVB multi-day pass covers GVB tram/bus/metro: [GVB prices](https://www.gvb.nl/en/prices). |

## Day 3 — Monday, 5 October 2026

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 09:00–10:00 | breakfast | Light self-catered vegan breakfast from market/grocery items bought Saturday, or coffee only if not dining. | Hotel room / nearby park | €15 estimate | Used to avoid non-vegan hotel breakfast uncertainty. Only buy clearly vegan items. |
| 10:30–12:30 | eco activity | Visit Hortus Botanicus, Amsterdam’s botanical garden. | Plantage Middenlaan | €27 estimate for 2 | Official site shows open 10:00–17:00: [Hortus Botanicus](https://www.dehortus.nl/en/). Ticket price approx. €13.50 adult from secondary evidence; verify on official site before booking. |
| 12:45–14:00 | lunch | Fully vegan lunch at SOIL Vegan Café, or another confirmed fully vegan branch if more convenient. | Amsterdam West / East depending route | €50 estimate | SOIL is a fully vegan restaurant per I amsterdam/search evidence and official hours from SOIL page: [SOIL West](https://www.soilvegancafe.com/soil-west). |
| 14:30–16:30 | sustainability activity | Visit De Ceuvel circular/sustainable waterfront site; have drinks/snacks only if the café’s vegan status is reconfirmed that day. | Amsterdam North | €25 estimate | I amsterdam describes Café de Ceuvel as sustainability-focused: [Café de Ceuvel](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/eating-and-drinking/cafes-and-bars/cafe-de-ceuvel). Search evidence said vegan/circular café, but official vegan status/hours were not fully verified. |
| 16:30–17:15 | transport | Ferry/tram/metro back toward hotel; free time. | Amsterdam North → Oud-West | Included in GVB pass | Check live route; GVB pass covers GVB services, but some ferry details should be checked locally: [GVB prices](https://www.gvb.nl/en/prices). |
| 18:30–20:00 | dinner | Dinner at Vegan Junk Food Bar for a casual plant-based meal. | Amsterdam centre / De Pijp branch | €70 estimate | I amsterdam states everything at Vegan Junk Food Bar is plant-based: [Vegan Junk Food Bar](https://www.iamsterdam.com/en/whats-on/calendar/eating-and-drinking/restaurants/vegan-junk-food-bar). |
| 20:15–21:15 | optional activity | Optional electric canal cruise if you want a low-emission sightseeing activity. | Central canals | €39 estimate for 2 | Search found electric-boat cruise evidence around €19.50 pp from third-party source; official operator/prices not firmly verified. Treat as optional and book only after checking operator sustainability claims. |

## Day 4 — Tuesday, 6 October 2026

| Time | Type | Plan | Location | Cost | Evidence/Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---:|---|
| 08:30–09:30 | breakfast | Simple vegan breakfast from groceries, or choose a confirmed fully vegan café open Tuesday. | Hotel / nearby | €20 estimate | Many identified vegan cafés have limited hours; Koffie ende Koeck appears Wed–Sun only, and Meatless District is closed Tuesday. Verify Tuesday options locally. |
| 09:30–10:30 | accommodation | Check out and store luggage if needed. | Conscious Hotel Amsterdam City / The Tire Station | Included | Hotel selected for sustainability profile: [Conscious Hotels](https://conscioushotels.com/stay/amsterdam-city). |
| 10:30–12:00 | activity | Final low-impact walk around Vondelpark or canal belt; avoid paid activities to keep departure day flexible. | Vondelpark / Oud-West | Free | Vondelpark is adjacent to hotel area per Conscious Hotels description: [hotel page](https://conscioushotels.com/stay/amsterdam-city). |
| 12:00–13:00 | lunch | Lunch at SOIL Vegan Café if convenient before train, or takeaway from a verified fully vegan venue. | Amsterdam West / centre | €45 estimate | SOIL official hours show Tue open under the general Mon–Sat schedule on searched page: [SOIL West](https://www.soilvegancafe.com/soil-west). Verify branch hours and kitchen times. |
| 13:00–14:00 | transport | Pick up luggage and travel to Amsterdam Centraal. | Hotel → Amsterdam Centraal | Included in GVB pass | GVB pass evidence: [GVB prices](https://www.gvb.nl/en/prices). |
| 14:00–17:00 | transport | Afternoon train back to Cologne Hbf. | Amsterdam Centraal → Cologne Hbf | €80–€140 estimate for 2 | NS International lists Amsterdam/Cologne rail from €20 one way and about 2h37 fastest: [NS International](https://www.nsinternational.com/en/train/cologne-amsterdam). Exact 2026 train times/fares unavailable. |
| 17:00 onward | trip end | Arrive in Cologne. | Cologne | €0 | Arrival time is an estimate pending live rail timetable. |

## Budget summary
- Return train Cologne–Amsterdam for 2: €160–€280 estimate; low fares start at €20 one way but realistic 2026 weekend fares may be higher.
- Eco-certified/sustainable hotel, 3 nights: €570–€750 estimate.
- GVB 4-day city transport tickets: €55 for 2, based on €27.50 pp official GVB price.
- Bike rental: €39–€49 estimate for 2 for 24h, depending on insurance.
- Vegan meals, cafés, market snacks: €450–€550 estimate.
- Activities:
  - Hortus Botanicus: approx. €27 for 2.
  - Optional electric canal cruise: approx. €39 for 2, if booked.
  - De Ceuvel / walks / parks: mostly free, drinks/snacks estimated above.
- Estimated total: **€1,340–€1,750 for two**, leaving roughly **€250–€660 buffer** within the €2,000 budget for fare increases, hotel taxes, reservations, luggage lockers, bike insurance, and spontaneous vegan purchases.

## Unscheduled or uncertain items
- No dedicated vegan festival or vegan market was found for 3–6 October 2026. The Great Vegan Gathering search result was for August 2026, not the trip dates.
- Exact 2026 Cologne–Amsterdam train departures and booking-class fares were not verifiable with Tavily alone.
- Hotel room availability and exact October 2026 rates at Conscious Hotel Amsterdam City / The Tire Station must be checked directly.
- De Ceuvel’s current café hours and fully vegan status need reconfirmation before treating it as a meal stop; it is scheduled mainly as a sustainability site.
- Electric canal cruise is optional because the sustainable/electric cruise price evidence came from third-party sources rather than a confirmed official operator page.
- Mr Watson evidence came mainly from HappyCow/social/search results; verify official opening status, location, and reservations before relying on it.

## Assumptions and gaps
- Assumed both travellers are comfortable cycling in Amsterdam traffic; if not, substitute extra walking, trams, and Vondelpark only.
- Assumed a double room at an eco-certified hotel is available within €190–€250/night; this requires live booking confirmation.
- Assumed the travellers will avoid non-vegan hotel breakfasts unless the hotel can confirm a fully plant-based breakfast prepared without animal products.
- All meal costs are estimates and exclude alcohol unless noted.
- This is a baseline plan using Tavily evidence only; final booking should verify official opening hours, menus, reservations, hotel certification, and rail tickets closer to the travel date.
